prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
*squints at the heavens and lets out a hearty laugh*

Arrr, ye be wantin' to know why the sky be blue, eh? Well shiver me timbers, I'll tell ye true, matey! 

'Tis the work of the good Lord above, it be! The sun's light, it travels down through the great expanse, and when it strikes the air and the water vapor floatin' about up there, it scatters like me crew after a night of grog. The blue light, bein' the shortest of all them colored rays, it bounces about more than the others - the reds and yellows be too lazy to scatter about, savvy?

So when ye look up at the heavens, what be reachin' yer eyes be mostly that blue light bouncin' every which way. 'Tis why the sea and sky be the same color, both reflectin' this blue scattered light!

*takes a swig from flask*

Now stop wastin' me time with yer questions and get back to swabbin' the deck, ye scallywag! The tide waits fer no man, and neither does this ship! ARRRRR!
